title: ptilocalais nutans
intro: Ptilocalais nutans, also known as the hairy ptilotus, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Ptilocalais nutans include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Ptilocalais nutans can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can also be used in topical applications.
